rationale.claude: Has the structural pieces of a competent letter (hook + experience + closing) but the paragraphs are dense walls with no breathing room. More concerning: the letter makes claims that violate the candidate's honest-limits truth table — specifically 'configuring systems, including per-machine Windows Update scheduling using Local Group Policy Editor, and setting up DNS zones on small-scale Windows servers' — Local GPO and DNS zone setup are both on the No-Exposure list per evidence.json. These are the exact overclaim shapes the integrity_guards layer should have stripped. The substantive prose around the overclaims is OK; if the overclaims came out the letter would score higher.

key_strengths: Strong opening hook tied to Solve Education's specific scope ('seamless IT operations and ensuring reliable technical support for users')
Concrete BEAT THE BOMB scope (Windows + Linux + help desk ticketing + Microsoft 365)
Uses the 75% guest-transition metric with the actual numbers (10 min → 2.5 min)
Closing 'I welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience can support your client's needs' is appropriately specific (refers to 'your client's needs' for an MSP-style role)
key_weaknesses: 'configuring systems, including per-machine Windows Update scheduling using Local Group Policy Editor' — LGP/gpedit is on Carlos's No-Exposure truth-limit list and should not appear
'setting up DNS zones on small-scale Windows servers' — DNS zone setup is also outside Carlos's documented scope (he troubleshoots DNS, doesn't author zones)
Paragraphs are dense walls (no breathing room) — paragraph 2 runs 80+ words as a single sentence-stack
Generic AI-tells: 'I am a hands-on troubleshooter with a strong service orientation' and 'I take ownership of issues'
'My commitment to technical competence, proactive communication, and user satisfaction' is the wrap-up boilerplate that adds no information
specific_fixes: Remove the LGP/Windows Update sentence entirely — replace with Carlos-defensible scope (Windows OS troubleshooting, PC hardware, ticketing, RustDesk)
Remove 'setting up DNS zones' — replace with 'TCP/IP and DNS troubleshooting' (the troubleshooting framing is truthful)
Break paragraph 2 into 2-3 shorter paragraphs with one idea each
Strip the 'commitment to technical competence' closing sentence
Add one concrete connection to Solve Education's mission (education-sector context) since the JD signals client-facing work
tier_definition_match: Matches mid (~7.5): the letter has the right shape but the truth-table violations are substantive issues. Without the LGP/DNS overclaims it would be a 7.8-8.0; with them it drops because a reader who pattern-matches on those claims could misread the candidate's scope. Above 'weak' because the hook and metrics work; below 'nearmiss' because the overclaims aren't polish issues — they're factual.
